msk.cluster.BrokerNodeGroupInfo.Docs: The setup to be used for brokers in the cluster.  AWS CloudFormation may replace the cluster when you update certain `BrokerNodeGroupInfo` properties. To understand the update behavior for your use case, you should review the child properties for [https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-properties-msk-cluster-brokernodegroupinfo.html#aws-properties-msk-cluster-brokernodegroupinfo-properties](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-properties-msk-cluster-brokernodegroupinfo.html#aws-properties-msk-cluster-brokernodegroupinfo-properties).

msk.cluster.ClientAuthentication.Docs: Includes information related to client authentication.

msk.cluster.ConfigurationInfo.Docs: The Amazon MSK configuration to use for the cluster.

msk.cluster.CurrentVersion.Docs: The version of the cluster that you want to update.

msk.cluster.EnhancedMonitoring.Docs: Specifies the level of monitoring for the MSK cluster. The possible values are `DEFAULT`, `PER_BROKER`, and `PER_TOPIC_PER_BROKER`.

msk.cluster.KafkaVersion.Docs: The version of Apache Kafka. For more information, see [Supported Apache Kafka versions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/msk/latest/developerguide/supported-kafka-versions.html) in the Amazon MSK Developer Guide.

msk.cluster.LoggingInfo.Docs: You can configure your Amazon MSK cluster to send broker logs to different destination types. This is a container for the configuration details related to broker logs.

msk.cluster.NumberOfBrokerNodes.Docs: The number of broker nodes you want in the Amazon MSK cluster. You can submit an update to increase the number of broker nodes in a cluster.

msk.cluster.Tags.Docs: A map of key:value pairs to apply to this resource. Both key and value are of type String.
